member.name: T:CodeLogic.CodeLogic
M:CodeLogic.CodeLogic.InitializeAsync(System.Action{CodeLogic.CodeLogicOptions})
member.summary: Initializes the framework. Parses CLI args, scaffolds directories on first run, loads CodeLogic.json, and prepares the LibraryManager. CLI args are merged on top of programmatic options (CLI wins on conflicts).
member.param: Optional delegate to set properties.
member.returns: An — check ShouldExit before continuing. A result with ShouldExit = true means a CLI flag like --version was handled.

member.name: M:CodeLogic.CodeLogic.RegisterApplication(CodeLogic.Framework.Application.IApplication)
member.summary: Registers the consuming application with the runtime. Must be called after and before .
member.param: The application instance implementing .

member.name: M:CodeLogic.CodeLogic.ConfigureAsync
member.summary: Configures the application: discovers DLL libraries, runs OnConfigureAsync, and generates/loads all config and localization files. Call after registering all libraries and the application.

member.name: M:CodeLogic.CodeLogic.StartAsync
member.summary: Starts all libraries and the application in the correct dependency order. Runs the full Configure → Initialize → Start sequence for libraries, then Initialize → Start for the application.

member.name: M:CodeLogic.CodeLogic.StopAsync
member.summary: Gracefully stops the application, then all plugins, then all libraries in reverse order. Called automatically on CTRL+C and process exit when is true.

member.name: M:CodeLogic.CodeLogic.ResetAsync
member.summary: Stops everything and resets the runtime to its uninitialized state. After calling this, may be called again. Primarily useful in integration tests.

member.name: M:CodeLogic.CodeLogic.GetHealthAsync
member.summary: Runs a health check across all libraries, plugins, and the application. Returns a with per-component status and an overall flag.

member.name: M:CodeLogic.CodeLogic.GetLibraryManager
member.summary: Returns the , or null if not yet initialized. Prefer the facade for day-to-day library access.

member.name: M:CodeLogic.CodeLogic.GetApplication
member.summary: Returns the registered , or null if none has been registered.

member.name: M:CodeLogic.CodeLogic.GetApplicationContext
member.summary: Returns the created during , or null if the application has not been configured.

member.name: M:CodeLogic.CodeLogic.GetEventBus
member.summary: Returns the shared instance. Available immediately after construction — even before .

member.name: M:CodeLogic.CodeLogic.GetOptions
member.summary: Returns the effective . Throws if called before .

member.name: M:CodeLogic.CodeLogic.GetConfiguration
member.summary: Returns the deserialized from CodeLogic.json. Throws if called before .

member.name: M:CodeLogic.CodeLogic.SetPluginManager(CodeLogic.Framework.Application.Plugins.PluginManager)
member.summary: Registers an app-managed PluginManager so it participates in health checks and graceful shutdown. Call after InitializeAsync.

member.name: M:CodeLogic.CodeLogic.GetPluginManager
member.summary: Returns the registered , or null if none has been registered.

member.name: T:CodeLogic.CodeLogicEnvironment
member.summary: Provides read-only runtime environment information. AppVersion is set by the consuming application via CodeLogicOptions.AppVersion.

member.name: P:CodeLogic.CodeLogicEnvironment.MachineName
member.summary: The machine name from the operating system.

member.name: P:CodeLogic.CodeLogicEnvironment.AppRootPath
member.summary: The application base directory (where the executable lives).

member.name: P:CodeLogic.CodeLogicEnvironment.AppVersion
member.summary: The application version. Set automatically from CodeLogicOptions.AppVersion during InitializeAsync. Defaults to "0.0.0".

member.name: P:CodeLogic.CodeLogicEnvironment.IsDebugging
member.summary: Returns true if a debugger is currently attached to this process.

member.name: P:CodeLogic.CodeLogicEnvironment.IsDevelopment
member.summary: Returns true when running in development mode. True if debugger is attached OR if this is a DEBUG build. Mirrors the framework's config file selection logic: IsDevelopment = true → CodeLogic.Development.json IsDevelopment = false → CodeLogic.json

member.name: T:CodeLogic.Core.Configuration.ConfigModelBase
member.summary: Base class for all CodeLogic configuration models. Inherit from this and use DataAnnotations attributes for validation. Override Validate() for custom validation logic.

member.name: M:CodeLogic.Core.Configuration.ConfigModelBase.Validate
member.summary: Validates this configuration model using DataAnnotations and any custom logic in overriding classes.

member.name: T:CodeLogic.Core.Configuration.ConfigSectionAttribute
member.summary: Marks a configuration model with a section name used in file naming. Example: [ConfigSection("database")] → config.database.json Without this attribute, the file name is config.json.

member.name: P:CodeLogic.Core.Configuration.ConfigSectionAttribute.SectionName
member.summary: The section name used as a suffix in the config file name. Example: "database" → config file is named config.database.json.

member.name: P:CodeLogic.Core.Configuration.ConfigSectionAttribute.Version
member.summary: Schema version for the config section. Informational — not used for migration. Default: 1.

member.name: M:CodeLogic.Core.Configuration.ConfigSectionAttribute.#ctor(System.String)
member.summary: Initializes the attribute with a section name.
member.param: The suffix for the config file name (e.g., "database").

member.name: T:CodeLogic.Core.Configuration.ConfigurationManager
member.summary: File-based JSON configuration manager for a single component. Thread-safe for concurrent reads; writes use file system atomicity.

member.name: T:CodeLogic.Core.Configuration.IConfigurationManager
member.summary: Manages configuration files for a single component (library, application, or plugin). Supports multiple config files per component: Register<MainConfig>() → config.json Register<DatabaseConfig>("db") → config.db.json

member.name: M:CodeLogic.Core.Configuration.IConfigurationManager.Register``1(System.String)
member.summary: Registers a config type for this component.

member.summary: Loads a config from disk. Generates defaults if missing when is true. Validates after load.

member.name: M:CodeLogic.Core.Configuration.IConfigurationManager.SaveAsync``1(``0)
member.summary: Saves config to disk. Validates before writing — throws if invalid.

member.summary: Reloads a specific config from disk and updates the in-memory instance. Safe for: log levels, intervals, pool sizes. NOT safe for: connection strings, paths, core settings (requires restart).

member.summary: Reloads all registered configs from disk.

member.summary: Returns the on-disk paths for all registered config files.

member.name: T:CodeLogic.Core.Events.IEvent
member.summary: Marker interface for all events in the CodeLogic event bus. Implement this on record or class types to make them publishable.

member.name: M:CodeLogic.Core.Events.IEventBus.Publish``1(``0)
member.summary: Publishes an event to all subscribers. Sync subscribers are called immediately on the calling thread. Async subscribers are fired-and-forgotten (not awaited). Use PublishAsync to await async subscribers.

member.summary: Publishes an event and awaits all subscribers (sync and async).

member.summary: Subscribes a synchronous handler for events of type T. Returns a subscription that can be disposed to unsubscribe.

member.summary: Subscribes an asynchronous handler for events of type T. Returns a subscription that can be disposed to unsubscribe.

member.summary: Represents an active event subscription. Dispose to unsubscribe and stop receiving events.

member.summary: The prefix used in the localization file name. Example: "homepoint" → file is named homepoint.en-US.json.

member.name: T:CodeLogic.Core.Logging.ILogger
member.summary: Provides structured log writing for a single named component (library, application, or plugin). Each component receives its own scoped logger from its context object. Log entries are written to disk (and optionally to the console) based on configured levels.

member.name: M:CodeLogic.Core.Logging.ILogger.Trace(System.String)
member.summary: Logs a trace-level message. The most verbose level — for fine-grained execution flow tracing. Disabled by default in all environments.
member.param: The message to log.

member.name: M:CodeLogic.Core.Logging.ILogger.Debug(System.String)
member.summary: Logs a debug-level message. Intended for diagnostic information during development. Enabled by default in development (CodeLogic.Development.json sets GlobalLevel to Debug).
member.param: The message to log.

member.name: M:CodeLogic.Core.Logging.ILogger.Info(System.String)
member.summary: Logs an informational message about normal application flow. Use for significant milestones (startup, connections established, jobs completed).
member.param: The message to log.

member.name: M:CodeLogic.Core.Logging.ILogger.Warning(System.String)
member.summary: Logs a warning — an unexpected condition that the application can recover from. Warnings do not halt execution but should be investigated. This is the default minimum log level in production.
member.param: The message to log.

member.name: M:CodeLogic.Core.Logging.ILogger.Error(System.String,System.Exception)
member.summary: Logs an error — a failure in the current operation. The application can continue but the operation did not succeed.
member.param: The message to log.
Optional exception to include in the log entry.

member.name: M:CodeLogic.Core.Logging.ILogger.Critical(System.String,System.Exception)
member.summary: Logs a critical error — a severe failure that may require immediate attention or will cause the application to shut down.
member.param: The message to log.
Optional exception to include in the log entry.

member.name: M:CodeLogic.Core.Logging.ILogger.Debug(System.String,System.Object[])
member.summary: Logs a formatted debug message using .
member.param: A composite format string.
Arguments to substitute into the format string.

member.name: M:CodeLogic.Core.Logging.ILogger.Info(System.String,System.Object[])
member.summary: Logs a formatted informational message using .
member.param: A composite format string.
Arguments to substitute into the format string.

member.name: M:CodeLogic.Core.Logging.ILogger.Warning(System.String,System.Object[])
member.summary: Logs a formatted warning message using .
member.param: A composite format string.
Arguments to substitute into the format string.

member.name: M:CodeLogic.Core.Logging.ILogger.Error(System.String,System.Object[])
member.summary: Logs a formatted error message using .
member.param: A composite format string.
Arguments to substitute into the format string.

member.name: F:CodeLogic.Core.Logging.LoggingMode.SingleFile
member.summary: Single rolling file per component. Default. Rolls when MaxFileSizeMb is reached. Keeps MaxRolledFiles old files. Files: component.log, component.1.log, component.2.log, ...

member.name: F:CodeLogic.Core.Logging.LoggingMode.DateFolder
member.summary: Files sorted into year/month/day subdirectories. Pattern configurable via FileNamePattern.

member.name: T:CodeLogic.Core.Logging.LoggingOptions
member.summary: Runtime logging configuration passed to the constructor. Produced from via ToLoggingOptions(). Can also be constructed manually for testing or custom components.

member.name: P:CodeLogic.Core.Logging.LoggingOptions.Mode
member.summary: Log file organization mode: rolling single file or date-sorted folders. Default: .

member.name: P:CodeLogic.Core.Logging.LoggingOptions.MaxFileSizeMb
member.summary: Maximum size of a single log file in megabytes before it rolls over. Applies only in mode. Default: 10.

member.name: P:CodeLogic.Core.Logging.LoggingOptions.MaxRolledFiles
member.summary: Number of old (rolled) log files to keep. Oldest are deleted when the limit is exceeded. Applies only in mode. Default: 5.

member.name: P:CodeLogic.Core.Logging.LoggingOptions.FileNamePattern
member.summary: File name pattern used in mode. Supports {date:format} and {level} tokens. Default: "{date:yyyy}/{date:MM}/{date:dd}/{level}.log".

member.name: P:CodeLogic.Core.Logging.LoggingOptions.GlobalLevel
member.summary: Minimum log level written to disk. Entries below this level are discarded. Default: .

member.name: P:CodeLogic.Core.Logging.LoggingOptions.EnableDebugMode
member.summary: When true, enables verbose debug output mode. Default: false.

member.name: P:CodeLogic.Core.Logging.LoggingOptions.CentralizedDebugLog
member.summary: When true, all component loggers also write to a shared centralized debug log file. Default: false.

member.name: P:CodeLogic.Core.Logging.LoggingOptions.CentralizedLogsPath
member.summary: Override path for the centralized debug log file. When null, defaults to {FrameworkRoot}/Framework/logs/debug.log.

member.name: P:CodeLogic.Core.Logging.LoggingOptions.EnableConsoleOutput
member.summary: When true, log entries are also written to the console (stdout). Default: false.

member.name: P:CodeLogic.Core.Logging.LoggingOptions.ConsoleMinimumLevel
member.summary: Minimum log level written to the console when is true. Default: .

member.name: P:CodeLogic.Core.Logging.LoggingOptions.TimestampFormat
member.summary: Timestamp format string applied to each log entry. Default: "yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss.fff".

member.name: P:CodeLogic.Core.Logging.LoggingOptions.IncludeMachineName
member.summary: When true, the machine name is prepended to each log entry. Useful when aggregating logs from multiple machines. Default: true.

member.name: M:CodeLogic.Core.Logging.LoggingOptions.CreateWithDebugDefaults
member.summary: Creates with debug-aware defaults. When a debugger is attached: sets to Debug, enables console output, and enables debug mode. When no debugger: returns quiet defaults (Warning level, no console). Individual properties can be overridden after calling this method.

member.name: T:CodeLogic.Core.Logging.LogLevel
member.summary: Severity levels for log messages, ordered from least to most severe. Configure the minimum level via in CodeLogic.json. Messages below the configured level are silently discarded.

member.name: F:CodeLogic.Core.Logging.LogLevel.Trace
member.summary: The most verbose level — extremely detailed execution traces. Use sparingly; can generate enormous log volume. Typically only enabled when diagnosing specific bugs in isolation.

member.name: F:CodeLogic.Core.Logging.LogLevel.Debug
member.summary: Diagnostic information useful during development and debugging. Enabled by default in development (via CodeLogic.Development.json). Disabled in production to avoid log noise.

member.name: F:CodeLogic.Core.Logging.LogLevel.Info
member.summary: Normal application events worth recording: startup, connections, job completions. Use for significant milestones that are expected during healthy operation.

member.name: F:CodeLogic.Core.Logging.LogLevel.Warning
member.summary: Unexpected conditions that the application can recover from. Examples: a retried operation, a missing optional resource, a deprecated API call. This is the default minimum level in production (CodeLogic.json).

member.name: F:CodeLogic.Core.Logging.LogLevel.Error
member.summary: A failure in the current operation that the application can continue running despite. Examples: a failed API call, a failed database query, an unhandled exception in a background task.

member.name: F:CodeLogic.Core.Logging.LogLevel.Critical
member.summary: A severe failure that typically requires immediate attention or signals imminent shutdown. Examples: startup failure, unrecoverable database corruption, out-of-memory conditions.

member.name: T:CodeLogic.Core.Logging.NullLogger
member.summary: A no-op logger that discards all log messages. Use for testing or when logging is optional.

member.name: F:CodeLogic.Core.Logging.NullLogger.Instance
member.summary: Shared singleton instance.

member.name: T:CodeLogic.Core.Results.Error
member.summary: Represents a structured error with a machine-readable code, human-readable message, optional details, and optional inner error for chaining. Use the static factory methods rather than constructing directly.

member.name: P:CodeLogic.Core.Results.Error.Code
member.summary: Machine-readable error code in "category.specific_issue" format. Examples: "user.not_found", "db.connection_failed". Use constants from for framework errors. Libraries should define their own codes following the same convention.

member.name: P:CodeLogic.Core.Results.Error.Message
member.summary: Human-readable description of the error. Suitable for logging and display.

member.name: P:CodeLogic.Core.Results.Error.Details
member.summary: Optional additional context (e.g., the field name, the invalid value, the file path).

member.name: P:CodeLogic.Core.Results.Error.InnerError
member.summary: Optional chained inner error that caused this error. Enables building error chains similar to exception inner exceptions.

member.name: M:CodeLogic.Core.Results.Error.NotFound(System.String,System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Creates an error representing a resource that could not be found. HTTP analog: 404 Not Found.

member.name: M:CodeLogic.Core.Results.Error.Validation(System.String,System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Creates an error representing a validation failure (invalid input data). HTTP analog: 422 Unprocessable Entity.

member.name: M:CodeLogic.Core.Results.Error.Internal(System.String,System.String,System.String,CodeLogic.Core.Results.Error)
member.summary: Creates an error representing an internal/unexpected failure. HTTP analog: 500 Internal Server Error.
member.param: Optional chained error that caused this failure.

member.name: M:CodeLogic.Core.Results.Error.Unauthorized(System.String,System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Creates an error representing an authentication failure. HTTP analog: 401 Unauthorized.

member.name: M:CodeLogic.Core.Results.Error.Conflict(System.String,System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Creates an error representing a conflict with current state (duplicate, concurrent modification). HTTP analog: 409 Conflict.

member.name: M:CodeLogic.Core.Results.Error.Timeout(System.String,System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Creates an error representing an operation that exceeded its time limit. HTTP analog: 408 Request Timeout / 504 Gateway Timeout.

member.name: M:CodeLogic.Core.Results.Error.Unavailable(System.String,System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Creates an error representing a service or dependency that is temporarily unavailable. HTTP analog: 503 Service Unavailable.

member.name: M:CodeLogic.Core.Results.Error.FromException(System.Exception,System.String)
member.summary: Wraps an exception as an internal error. The exception message becomes the error message; the exception type name becomes the details.
member.param: The exception to wrap.
Error code to use. Default: "internal.exception".

member.name: M:CodeLogic.Core.Results.Error.WithInner(CodeLogic.Core.Results.Error)
member.summary: Returns a new Error with the given inner error attached. Use this to chain errors without mutating the original.
member.param: The root cause error.

member.name: M:CodeLogic.Core.Results.Error.WithDetails(System.String)
member.summary: Returns a new Error with the given details string. Use this to add context without mutating the original.
member.param: Additional context to attach.

member.name: M:CodeLogic.Core.Results.Error.ToString
member.summary: Returns a string representation: [code] message (details) → innerError.

member.name: T:CodeLogic.Core.Results.ErrorCode
member.summary: Well-known error code constants. Format: "category.specific_issue". Libraries may define their own codes following the same convention. These constants are intended to be used with the factory methods.

member.name: F:CodeLogic.Core.Results.ErrorCode.NotFound
member.summary: The requested resource does not exist.

member.name: F:CodeLogic.Core.Results.ErrorCode.AlreadyExists
member.summary: A resource with this identity already exists.

member.name: F:CodeLogic.Core.Results.ErrorCode.InvalidArgument
member.summary: An argument provided to an operation is invalid.

member.name: F:CodeLogic.Core.Results.ErrorCode.InvalidState
member.summary: The operation cannot be performed in the current state.

member.name: F:CodeLogic.Core.Results.ErrorCode.NotSupported
member.summary: The requested operation is not supported by this implementation.

member.name: F:CodeLogic.Core.Results.ErrorCode.Cancelled
member.summary: The operation was cancelled before completion.

member.name: F:CodeLogic.Core.Results.ErrorCode.Timeout
member.summary: The operation exceeded its time limit.

member.name: F:CodeLogic.Core.Results.ErrorCode.Internal
member.summary: An unexpected internal error occurred.

member.name: F:CodeLogic.Core.Results.ErrorCode.Unauthorized
member.summary: The caller is not authenticated.

member.name: F:CodeLogic.Core.Results.ErrorCode.Forbidden
member.summary: The caller is authenticated but does not have permission for this operation.

member.name: F:CodeLogic.Core.Results.ErrorCode.ValidationFailed
member.summary: One or more validation rules failed.

member.name: F:CodeLogic.Core.Results.ErrorCode.RequiredMissing
member.summary: A required field is missing or null.

member.name: F:CodeLogic.Core.Results.ErrorCode.InvalidFormat

member.name: T:CodeLogic.Framework.Application.IApplication
member.summary:
Interface for the consuming application to participate in the CodeLogic lifecycle. Register via before ConfigureAsync().
The application follows the same 4-phase lifecycle as libraries, but with important timing:
is called during ConfigureAsync(), before libraries start.
and are called during StartAsync(), AFTER all libraries are running.
is called during StopAsync(), BEFORE libraries are stopped.
This ordering guarantees that library services are available when the application starts, and the application is fully stopped before its dependencies are torn down.

member.name: P:CodeLogic.Framework.Application.IApplication.Manifest
member.summary: Application metadata: ID, name, version, description, and author. Used for display in logs and health reports.

member.name: M:CodeLogic.Framework.Application.IApplication.OnConfigureAsync(CodeLogic.Framework.Application.ApplicationContext)
member.summary: Phase 1 — Configuration. Called during ConfigureAsync(), before libraries start. Register config and localization models with the context managers. The framework generates missing config files and loads all registered configs immediately after this method returns. Do NOT read config values here.
member.param: The application context providing scoped services and paths.

member.name: M:CodeLogic.Framework.Application.IApplication.OnInitializeAsync(CodeLogic.Framework.Application.ApplicationContext)
member.summary: Phase 2 — Initialization. Called during StartAsync(), after all libraries are running. At this point, all library services are available. Set up application services, resolve dependencies from libraries, and prepare for operation. Throw an exception to abort startup.
member.param: The application context with loaded config and localization.

member.name: M:CodeLogic.Framework.Application.IApplication.OnStartAsync(CodeLogic.Framework.Application.ApplicationContext)
member.summary: Phase 3 — Start. Called during StartAsync(), immediately after . Start background services, subscribe to events, open connections, begin processing.
member.param: The application context (same instance as Initialize).

member.name: M:CodeLogic.Framework.Application.IApplication.OnStopAsync
member.summary: Phase 4 — Stop. Called during StopAsync() BEFORE libraries are stopped. Flush pending work, stop background tasks, close connections, and release resources. After this returns, the framework will stop all libraries.

member.name: M:CodeLogic.Framework.Application.IApplication.HealthCheckAsync
member.summary: Returns the current health of the application. Called by the framework during scheduled health checks and the --health CLI flag. The default implementation always returns Healthy — override to add real checks.
member.returns: A reflecting the application's current operational state.

member.name: T:CodeLogic.Framework.Application.Plugins.IPlugin
member.summary:
Interface for hot-loadable CodeLogic plugins. Plugins are app-managed: the consuming application decides what to load via . Plugins follow the same 4-phase lifecycle as libraries but are loaded into isolated instances, enabling true hot-reload without restarting the host.
Plugin assemblies are discovered from {FrameworkRoot}/Plugins/*.Plugin/*.Plugin.dll.

member.name: P:CodeLogic.Framework.Application.Plugins.IPlugin.Manifest
member.summary: Plugin metadata: ID, name, version, description, author, and minimum framework version.

member.name: P:CodeLogic.Framework.Application.Plugins.IPlugin.State
member.summary: Current lifecycle state of this plugin instance. Transitions from through to .

member.name: M:CodeLogic.Framework.Application.Plugins.IPlugin.OnConfigureAsync(CodeLogic.Framework.Application.Plugins.PluginContext)
member.summary: Phase 1 — Configuration. Called first when the plugin is loaded. Register config and localization models with the context managers. The framework generates missing config files and loads all registered configs immediately after this method returns. Do NOT read config values here.
member.param: The plugin context providing scoped services and paths.

member.name: M:CodeLogic.Framework.Application.Plugins.IPlugin.OnInitializeAsync(CodeLogic.Framework.Application.Plugins.PluginContext)
member.summary: Phase 2 — Initialization. Called after configs and localization are loaded. Set up plugin services, validate configuration, and prepare resources. Throw an exception here to abort loading — the plugin will transition to .
member.param: The plugin context with loaded config and localization.

member.name: M:CodeLogic.Framework.Application.Plugins.IPlugin.OnStartAsync(CodeLogic.Framework.Application.Plugins.PluginContext)
member.summary: Phase 3 — Start. Called after initialization completes. Start background services, subscribe to events, and begin processing. After this returns, the plugin is considered fully operational. A is published on the event bus.
member.param: The plugin context (same instance as Initialize).

member.name: M:CodeLogic.Framework.Application.Plugins.IPlugin.OnUnloadAsync
member.summary: Phase 4 — Unload. Called when the plugin is being unloaded (shutdown, hot-reload, or manual unload). Stop background tasks, close connections, flush buffers, and release resources. After this returns, the plugin's assembly will be unloaded from memory.

member.name: M:CodeLogic.Framework.Application.Plugins.IPlugin.HealthCheckAsync
member.summary: Returns the current health status of this plugin. Called during scheduled health checks and the --health CLI flag.

member.name: T:CodeLogic.Framework.Application.Plugins.PluginContext
member.summary: Context provided to plugins at every lifecycle phase. Full parity with — includes Config, Localization, Events, and Logger. All paths and services are scoped to this specific plugin's directory.

member.summary: The plugin's unique ID. Matches .

member.summary: Absolute path to this plugin's localization directory. Per-culture JSON files (e.g., strings.en-US.json) are stored here.

member.summary: Absolute path to this plugin's logs directory. The scoped writes here.

member.summary: Absolute path to this plugin's data directory. Use this for persistent plugin-owned files.

member.summary: A scoped logger configured for this plugin. Log entries are written to and tagged with the plugin ID.

member.summary: Configuration manager scoped to this plugin's .

member.name: P:CodeLogic.Framework.Application.Plugins.PluginContext.Localization
member.summary: Localization manager scoped to this plugin's .

member.name: P:CodeLogic.Framework.Application.Plugins.PluginContext.Events
member.summary: The shared framework event bus. Plugins share the same event bus as libraries and the application.

member.name: T:CodeLogic.Framework.Application.Plugins.PluginLoadContext
member.summary: Isolated AssemblyLoadContext for each plugin. Allows true hot-unload by releasing the assembly from memory. isCollectible=true enables GC collection after Unload().

member.name: M:CodeLogic.Framework.Application.Plugins.PluginManager.RegisterInProcessPluginAsync(CodeLogic.Framework.Application.Plugins.IPlugin,CodeLogic.Framework.Application.Plugins.PluginContext)
member.summary:
Registers an in-process plugin (already instantiated and running) with the PluginManager so that it participates in health checks, , and graceful shutdown via .
Use this when hosting a plugin in the same process/assembly (e.g. demos, tests) rather than loading it from a separate DLL via . The caller is responsible for running the full 4-phase lifecycle before calling this method.
member.param: The already-started plugin instance to register.
The the plugin was started with.

member.name: T:CodeLogic.Framework.Libraries.ILibrary
member.summary:
Interface for a CodeLogic library — a self-contained, reusable service component. Libraries participate in a strict 4-phase lifecycle managed by . Libraries are started in dependency order and stopped in reverse order.
Lifecycle phases (in order):
— Register configs and localization models.
— Set up services using loaded config.
— Start background tasks, open connections.
— Gracefully stop and release resources.

member.name: P:CodeLogic.Framework.Libraries.ILibrary.Manifest
member.summary: Metadata describing this library: ID, name, version, dependencies, etc. Used by the LibraryManager for discovery, dependency resolution, and display.

member.name: M:CodeLogic.Framework.Libraries.ILibrary.OnConfigureAsync(CodeLogic.Framework.Libraries.LibraryContext)
member.summary: Phase 1 — Configuration. Called first, before any other phase. Register config and localization models with the context managers. The framework generates missing config files and loads all registered configs immediately after this method returns. Do NOT access config values here — they are not loaded yet.
member.param: The library context providing scoped services and paths.

member.name: M:CodeLogic.Framework.Libraries.ILibrary.OnInitializeAsync(CodeLogic.Framework.Libraries.LibraryContext)
member.summary: Phase 2 — Initialization. Called after all configs and localizations are loaded. Set up services, validate configuration, establish connections, and prepare resources. Throw an exception here to abort startup — the library will transition to .
member.param: The library context with loaded config and localization.

member.name: M:CodeLogic.Framework.Libraries.ILibrary.OnStartAsync(CodeLogic.Framework.Libraries.LibraryContext)
member.summary: Phase 3 — Start. Called after all libraries have been initialized. Start background services, open long-lived connections, begin processing. After this method returns, the library is considered fully operational. A is published on the event bus.
member.param: The library context (same instance as Initialize).

member.name: M:CodeLogic.Framework.Libraries.ILibrary.OnStopAsync
member.summary: Phase 4 — Stop. Called in reverse start order during graceful shutdown. Stop background tasks, close connections, flush buffers, release resources. Exceptions here are logged but do not prevent other libraries from stopping. A is published on the event bus.

member.name: M:CodeLogic.Framework.Libraries.ILibrary.HealthCheckAsync
member.summary: Returns the current health status of this library. Called on a timer (see ) and on demand via the --health CLI flag or . Implement this to report database connectivity, queue depth, or any other meaningful status.
member.returns: A indicating Healthy, Degraded, or Unhealthy.

member.name: T:CodeLogic.Framework.Libraries.LibraryContext
member.summary: Context provided to each library at every lifecycle phase. All paths and services are scoped to this specific library. The same instance is passed to and — store it if you need it during OnStopAsync.

member.name: P:CodeLogic.Framework.Libraries.LibraryContext.LibraryId
member.summary: The library's unique ID (e.g., "CL.SQLite"). Matches .

member.name: P:CodeLogic.Framework.Libraries.LibraryContext.LibraryDirectory
member.summary: Absolute path to this library's root directory under Libraries/. Example: CodeLogic/Libraries/CL.SQLite/

member.name: P:CodeLogic.Framework.Libraries.LibraryContext.ConfigDirectory
member.summary: Absolute path to this library's config directory. Config files (config.json, config.db.json, etc.) are stored here. Same as by default.

member.name: P:CodeLogic.Framework.Libraries.LibraryContext.LocalizationDirectory
member.summary: Absolute path to this library's localization directory. Per-culture JSON files (e.g., strings.en-US.json) are stored here. Example: CodeLogic/Libraries/CL.SQLite/localization/

member.name: P:CodeLogic.Framework.Libraries.LibraryContext.LogsDirectory
member.summary: Absolute path to this library's logs directory. The scoped writes here. Example: CodeLogic/Libraries/CL.SQLite/logs/

member.name: P:CodeLogic.Framework.Libraries.LibraryContext.DataDirectory
member.summary: Absolute path to this library's data directory. Use this for persistent library-owned files (databases, caches, exports, etc.). Example: CodeLogic/Libraries/CL.SQLite/data/

member.name: P:CodeLogic.Framework.Libraries.LibraryContext.Logger
member.summary: A scoped logger configured for this library. Log entries are written to and tagged with the library ID.

member.name: P:CodeLogic.Framework.Libraries.LibraryContext.Configuration
member.summary: Configuration manager scoped to this library's . Use this to register, load, save, and reload config models.

member.name: P:CodeLogic.Framework.Libraries.LibraryContext.Localization
member.summary: Localization manager scoped to this library's . Use this to register and retrieve localized string models.

member.name: P:CodeLogic.Framework.Libraries.LibraryContext.Events
member.summary: The shared framework event bus. All libraries and the application share the same instance. Use this to publish and subscribe to events.

member.name: T:CodeLogic.Framework.Libraries.LibraryManager
member.summary: Manages the full lifecycle of all registered CL.* libraries. Thread-safe: lifecycle methods are serialized via SemaphoreSlim.

member.name: M:CodeLogic.Framework.Libraries.LibraryManager.LoadLibraryAsync``1
member.summary: Manually registers a library by type. Dependency validation is deferred to ConfigureAllAsync which runs ValidateDependencies after all libraries are registered.

member.name: M:CodeLogic.Framework.Libraries.LibraryManager.ForceRegenerateAllConfigsAsync(System.String[])
member.summary: Force-regenerates config defaults for all configured libraries, overwriting existing files. Only runs for libraries that have already completed their Configure phase (Context is set). Pass an empty array to include all libraries; pass specific IDs to scope generation.

member.name: T:CodeLogic.Framework.Libraries.LibraryManifest
member.summary: Describes a library's identity and requirements. Returned by and used by for discovery, dependency resolution, and display.

member.name: P:CodeLogic.Framework.Libraries.LibraryManifest.Id
member.summary: Unique identifier for this library. By convention, prefixed with CL. (e.g., "CL.SQLite", "CL.Mail"). The ID determines the library's directory name under Libraries/.

member.name: P:CodeLogic.Framework.Libraries.LibraryManifest.Name
member.summary: Human-readable display name (e.g., "SQLite Library"). Shown in console output, health reports, and logs.

member.name: P:CodeLogic.Framework.Libraries.LibraryManifest.Version
member.summary: Semantic version string (e.g., "1.0.0"). Checked during dependency validation when a is specified.

member.name: P:CodeLogic.Framework.Libraries.LibraryManifest.Description
member.summary: Optional description of what this library provides. Shown in --info output.

member.name: P:CodeLogic.Framework.Libraries.LibraryManifest.Author
member.summary: Optional author or team name.

member.name: P:CodeLogic.Framework.Libraries.LibraryManifest.Dependencies
member.summary: Other libraries this library depends on. Used for topological sort during startup. Required dependencies that are missing will prevent startup. Optional dependencies that are missing are silently skipped.

member.name: P:CodeLogic.Framework.Libraries.LibraryManifest.Tags
member.summary: Optional tags for categorization and filtering. Not used by the framework at runtime.

member.name: T:CodeLogic.Framework.Libraries.LibraryDependency
member.summary: Declares a dependency on another library within a . Use the factory methods (, ) rather than constructing directly.

member.name: P:CodeLogic.Framework.Libraries.LibraryDependency.Id
member.summary: The ID of the required library (e.g., "CL.SQLite"). Must match the of the depended-upon library exactly.

member.name: P:CodeLogic.Framework.Libraries.LibraryDependency.MinVersion
member.summary: Minimum acceptable version of the dependency (e.g., "1.2.0"). When set, the framework validates that the loaded library meets this requirement. When null, any version is accepted.

member.name: P:CodeLogic.Framework.Libraries.LibraryDependency.IsOptional
member.summary: When true, a missing dependency does not block startup. When false (default), a missing dependency throws during dependency validation.

member.name: M:CodeLogic.Framework.Libraries.LibraryDependency.Required(System.String)
member.summary: Creates a required dependency with no minimum version constraint.

member.name: M:CodeLogic.Framework.Libraries.LibraryDependency.Required(System.String,System.String)

member.summary: Creates an optional dependency — startup continues even if the library is absent.

member.name: M:CodeLogic.Framework.Libraries.LibraryDependency.Optional(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Creates an optional dependency with a minimum version constraint.

The minimum acceptable version if the library is present.

member.name: T:CodeLogic.Framework.Libraries.LibraryDependencyAttribute
member.summary: Applied to library classes to declare dependencies on other libraries. Equivalent to adding entries to . Can be applied multiple times for multiple dependencies.

member.name: P:CodeLogic.Framework.Libraries.LibraryDependencyAttribute.Id
member.summary: The ID of the library this library depends on (e.g., "CL.SQLite").

member.name: P:CodeLogic.Framework.Libraries.LibraryDependencyAttribute.MinVersion
member.summary: Minimum required version, or null for any version.

member.name: P:CodeLogic.Framework.Libraries.LibraryDependencyAttribute.IsOptional
member.summary: When true, startup continues even if this dependency is not loaded.

member.name: M:CodeLogic.Framework.Libraries.LibraryDependencyAttribute.ToDependency
member.summary: Converts this attribute to a record.

member.name: T:CodeLogic.Framework.Libraries.LibraryState
member.summary: Tracks the lifecycle state of a loaded library. States advance sequentially through the normal lifecycle. can be set from any phase when an exception occurs.

member.name: F:CodeLogic.Framework.Libraries.LibraryState.Loaded
member.summary: Added to but has not run yet.

member.name: F:CodeLogic.Framework.Libraries.LibraryState.Configured
member.summary: completed and config/localization files were loaded.

member.name: F:CodeLogic.Framework.Libraries.LibraryState.Initialized
member.summary: completed successfully.

member.name: F:CodeLogic.Framework.Libraries.LibraryState.Started
member.summary: completed — the library is fully operational.

member.name: F:CodeLogic.Framework.Libraries.LibraryState.Stopped
member.summary: completed — the library has shut down cleanly.

member.name: F:CodeLogic.Framework.Libraries.LibraryState.Failed
member.summary: An exception occurred during any lifecycle phase. The library is not operational.

member.name: T:CodeLogic.Framework.Libraries.LoadedLibrary
member.summary: Internal record of a library registered with LibraryManager.

member.name: P:CodeLogic.Framework.Libraries.LoadedLibrary.Context
member.summary: Context assigned after OnConfigureAsync completes.

member.name: P:CodeLogic.Framework.Libraries.LoadedLibrary.State
member.summary: Current lifecycle state.

member.name: P:CodeLogic.Framework.Libraries.LoadedLibrary.FailureException
member.summary: Exception if State == Failed.

member.name: T:CodeLogic.Libraries
member.summary: Static accessor for loaded libraries. Provides a convenient shorthand over .

member.name: M:CodeLogic.Libraries.Get``1
member.summary: Retrieves a loaded library by its concrete type. Returns null if no library of type is loaded.
member.typeparam: The library type, which must implement .
member.returns: The library instance, or null if not loaded.
member.exception: Thrown if has not been called yet.

member.name: M:CodeLogic.Libraries.LoadAsync``1
member.summary: Dynamically loads a library by type and registers it with the LibraryManager. Call this between and to register libraries before the configure/start sequence runs.
member.typeparam: The library type. Must implement and have a public parameterless constructor.
member.returns: True if the library was loaded successfully; false if it was already registered.
member.exception: Thrown if has not been called yet.

member.name: M:CodeLogic.Libraries.Get(System.String)
member.summary: Retrieves a loaded library by its string ID (e.g., "CL.SQLite"). Returns null if no library with that ID is loaded.
member.param: The library ID as declared in its .
member.exception: Thrown if has not been called yet.

member.name: M:CodeLogic.Libraries.GetAll
member.summary: Returns all currently loaded libraries. Safe to call before — returns an empty sequence rather than throwing. Useful for diagnostics or iterating all libraries without knowing their types.

member.name: T:CodeLogic.CodeLogicConfiguration
member.summary: Strongly-typed representation of CodeLogic.json (or CodeLogic.Development.json). Deserialized by the runtime during . Do not instantiate directly — the runtime owns this object.

member.name: P:CodeLogic.CodeLogicConfiguration.Framework
member.summary: Framework identity information (name and version).

member.name: P:CodeLogic.CodeLogicConfiguration.Logging
member.summary: Logging behavior: file mode, levels, console output, and format.

member.name: P:CodeLogic.CodeLogicConfiguration.Localization
member.summary: Localization settings: default culture, supported cultures, and template generation.

member.name: P:CodeLogic.CodeLogicConfiguration.Libraries
member.summary: Library discovery settings: naming pattern and dependency resolution.

member.name: P:CodeLogic.CodeLogicConfiguration.HealthChecks
member.summary: Scheduled health check settings: enabled flag and interval.

member.name: T:CodeLogic.FrameworkConfig
member.summary: Framework identity block within . Informational only — not used at runtime for any behavior decisions.

member.name: P:CodeLogic.FrameworkConfig.Name
member.summary: Display name for the framework. Default: "CodeLogic".

member.name: P:CodeLogic.FrameworkConfig.Version
member.summary: Framework version string. Default: "3.0.0".

member.name: T:CodeLogic.LoggingConfig
member.summary: Controls log file behavior, log levels, console output, and format. Maps to the "logging" section in CodeLogic.json.

member.name: P:CodeLogic.LoggingConfig.Mode
member.summary: Log file mode: "singleFile" (default, rolling by size) or "dateFolder" (files sorted into year/month/day directories).

member.name: P:CodeLogic.LoggingConfig.MaxFileSizeMb
member.summary: Maximum size of a single log file in megabytes before it rolls over. Applies only in singleFile mode. Default: 10.

member.name: P:CodeLogic.LoggingConfig.MaxRolledFiles
member.summary: Maximum number of rolled (archived) log files to keep. Oldest files are deleted when the limit is exceeded. Default: 5.

member.name: P:CodeLogic.LoggingConfig.FileNamePattern
member.summary: File name pattern used in dateFolder mode. Supports {date:format} and {level} tokens. Default: "{date:yyyy}/{date:MM}/{date:dd}/{level}.log".

member.name: P:CodeLogic.LoggingConfig.GlobalLevel
member.summary: Minimum log level written to disk. Values: Trace, Debug, Info, Warning, Error, Critical. Log entries below this level are discarded. Default: "Warning". Override to "Debug" in CodeLogic.Development.json for development.

member.name: P:CodeLogic.LoggingConfig.EnableConsoleOutput
member.summary: When true, logs are also written to the console (stdout). Default: false. Set to true in CodeLogic.Development.json for development.

member.name: P:CodeLogic.LoggingConfig.ConsoleMinimumLevel
member.summary: Minimum log level written to the console when is true. Default: "Debug".

member.name: P:CodeLogic.LoggingConfig.EnableDebugMode
member.summary: When true, enables verbose debug logging mode for detailed diagnostic output. Default: false.

member.name: P:CodeLogic.LoggingConfig.CentralizedDebugLog
member.summary: When true, all library loggers write to a shared central debug log file in addition to their own log files. Useful for correlating events across components. Default: false.

member.name: P:CodeLogic.LoggingConfig.CentralizedLogsPath
member.summary: Override path for the centralized debug log file. When null, defaults to {FrameworkRoot}/Framework/logs/debug.log.

member.name: P:CodeLogic.LoggingConfig.IncludeMachineName
member.summary: When true, the machine name is prepended to each log entry. Useful when log files are aggregated from multiple machines. Default: true.

member.name: P:CodeLogic.LoggingConfig.TimestampFormat
member.summary: Timestamp format string used for each log entry. Default: "yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss.fff".

member.name: M:CodeLogic.LoggingConfig.GetGlobalLogLevel
member.summary: Parses to a enum value. Falls back to if the string is invalid.

member.name: M:CodeLogic.LoggingConfig.GetConsoleLogLevel
member.summary: Parses to a enum value. Falls back to if the string is invalid.

member.name: M:CodeLogic.LoggingConfig.ToLoggingOptions
member.summary: Converts this configuration section to a instance suitable for passing to the constructor.

member.name: T:CodeLogic.LocalizationConfig
member.summary: Localization settings within . Maps to the "localization" section in CodeLogic.json.

member.name: P:CodeLogic.LocalizationConfig.DefaultCulture
member.summary: The default culture used when a requested culture is not available. All string models must have a complete translation for this culture. Default: "en-US".

member.name: P:CodeLogic.LocalizationConfig.SupportedCultures
member.summary: List of all culture codes that will be loaded and supported. Template files are generated for each culture on first run. Default: ["en-US"].

member.name: P:CodeLogic.LocalizationConfig.AutoGenerateTemplates
member.summary: When true, missing localization template files are auto-generated with default strings. Default: true.

member.name: T:CodeLogic.LibrariesConfig
member.summary: Library discovery and resolution settings within . Maps to the "libraries" section in CodeLogic.json.

member.name: P:CodeLogic.LibrariesConfig.DiscoveryPattern
member.summary: Glob pattern used to discover library subdirectories under Libraries/. Only directories matching this pattern are loaded. Default: "CL.*".

member.name: P:CodeLogic.LibrariesConfig.EnableDependencyResolution
member.summary: When true, libraries are sorted by their declared dependencies before startup (topological sort). When false, libraries start in registration order. Default: true.

member.name: T:CodeLogic.HealthChecksConfig
member.summary: Scheduled health check settings within . Maps to the "healthChecks" section in CodeLogic.json.

member.name: P:CodeLogic.HealthChecksConfig.Enabled
member.summary: When true, the framework runs health checks on a timer after startup. Results are published as on the event bus. Default: true.

member.name: P:CodeLogic.HealthChecksConfig.IntervalSeconds
member.summary: How often (in seconds) to run scheduled health checks across all libraries and plugins. Default: 30.

member.name: T:CodeLogic.CodeLogicOptions
member.summary: Configuration options for the CodeLogic framework, passed to . Control paths, app identity, config-generation behavior, and shutdown handling.

member.name: P:CodeLogic.CodeLogicOptions.FrameworkRootPath
member.summary: Root directory for framework files (CodeLogic.json, Libraries/, logs/). Relative to the application base directory (). Default: "CodeLogic".

member.name: P:CodeLogic.CodeLogicOptions.ApplicationRootPath
member.summary: Root directory for application files (config, localization, logs, data). Defaults to {FrameworkRootPath}/Application when null. Set this to an absolute or relative path to decouple application data from the framework root.

member.name: P:CodeLogic.CodeLogicOptions.AppVersion
member.summary: Version string reported in logs, health checks, and --version CLI output. Set this to your application's semantic version (e.g., "1.2.3"). Stored in after initialization.

member.name: P:CodeLogic.CodeLogicOptions.GenerateConfigs
member.summary: When true, missing config files are auto-generated with defaults on startup. Set to false to require all config files to exist before startup (strict mode). Default: true.

member.name: P:CodeLogic.CodeLogicOptions.GenerateConfigsForce
member.summary: When true, ALL config files are regenerated even if they already exist. This is destructive — existing configs will be overwritten with defaults. Use with caution; typically triggered by the --generate-configs-force CLI flag.

member.name: P:CodeLogic.CodeLogicOptions.GenerateConfigsFor
P:CodeLogic.CodeLogicOptions.ExitAfterGenerate
member.summary: When true, the process exits after generating configs instead of continuing startup. Default: false. Useful for CI pipelines that generate configs and then exit.

member.name: P:CodeLogic.CodeLogicOptions.DryRun
member.summary: When true, the framework runs only the Configure phase, validates existing config and localization files, prints any config files that would be generated or overwritten, and then exits without starting any libraries or the application. Useful for CI/CD pipelines that validate startup inputs without mutating the working tree. Triggered by the --dry-run CLI flag.

member.name: P:CodeLogic.CodeLogicOptions.HandleShutdownSignals
member.summary: When true, hooks and to call StopAsync() automatically. Set to false if your host manages the application lifetime externally (e.g., a Windows Service). Default: true.

member.name: M:CodeLogic.CodeLogicOptions.GetFrameworkPath
member.summary: Returns the absolute path to the framework root directory. Combines with .

member.name: M:CodeLogic.CodeLogicOptions.GetCodeLogicConfigPath
member.summary: Returns the absolute path to the main framework configuration file (CodeLogic.json). Located at {FrameworkRoot}/Framework/CodeLogic.json.

member.name: M:CodeLogic.CodeLogicOptions.GetCodeLogicDevelopmentConfigPath
member.summary: Path to the development config replacement file (CodeLogic.Development.json). Loaded instead of CodeLogic.json when the runtime is in development mode. Add this file to .gitignore — it is per-machine and should never be committed.

member.name: M:CodeLogic.CodeLogicOptions.GetLibrariesPath
member.summary: Returns the absolute path to the Libraries root directory. Each library lives in a subdirectory named by its ID (e.g., Libraries/CL.SQLite/).

member.name: M:CodeLogic.CodeLogicOptions.GetLibraryPath(System.String)
member.summary: Returns the absolute path to a specific library's directory. Normalizes the ID so both "SQLite" and "CL.SQLite" resolve to the same path.
member.param: The library ID with or without the "CL." prefix.

member.name: M:CodeLogic.CodeLogicOptions.GetApplicationPath
member.summary: Returns the absolute path to the application root directory. Uses if set; otherwise defaults to {FrameworkRoot}/Application.

member.name: M:CodeLogic.CodeLogicOptions.GetApplicationConfigPath
member.summary: Returns the absolute path to the application config directory. Config files (config.json, config.db.json, etc.) are stored here.

member.name: M:CodeLogic.CodeLogicOptions.GetApplicationLocalizationPath
member.summary: Returns the absolute path to the application localization directory. Per-culture JSON files (e.g., strings.en-US.json) are stored here.

member.name: M:CodeLogic.CodeLogicOptions.GetApplicationLogsPath
member.summary: Returns the absolute path to the application logs directory. The application logger writes to this directory.

member.name: M:CodeLogic.CodeLogicOptions.GetApplicationDataPath
member.summary: Returns the absolute path to the application data directory. Use this for persistent application-owned files (databases, caches, etc.).

member.name: M:CodeLogic.CodeLogicOptions.GetPluginsPath
member.summary: Returns the absolute path to the Plugins directory. Plugin subdirectories matching *.Plugin are discovered here.

member.name: T:CodeLogic.HealthReport
member.summary: Aggregated health report from all running libraries, plugins, and the application. Produced by . is true only when ALL components are Healthy.

member.name: P:CodeLogic.HealthReport.IsHealthy
member.summary: Overall health flag. True only when every library, plugin, and the application are Healthy. False if any component is Degraded or Unhealthy.

member.name: P:CodeLogic.HealthReport.CheckedAt
member.summary: UTC timestamp when this report was generated.

member.name: P:CodeLogic.HealthReport.MachineName
member.summary: The machine name from .

member.name: P:CodeLogic.HealthReport.AppVersion
member.summary: The application version from .

member.name: P:CodeLogic.HealthReport.Libraries
member.summary: Health status for each library, keyed by library ID (e.g., "CL.SQLite"). Only includes libraries in the state.

member.name: P:CodeLogic.HealthReport.Plugins
member.summary: Health status for each plugin, keyed by plugin ID. Only includes plugins in the state.

member.name: P:CodeLogic.HealthReport.Application
member.summary: Health status from the registered application, or null if no application is registered.

member.name: M:CodeLogic.HealthReport.ToJson
member.summary: Serializes the report to indented JSON, suitable for monitoring systems or REST endpoints.

member.name: M:CodeLogic.HealthReport.ToConsoleString
member.summary: Formats the report as a human-readable console table. Suitable for terminal output after the --health CLI flag.

member.name: T:CodeLogic.ICodeLogicRuntime
member.summary: The core runtime contract for the CodeLogic framework. Implemented by and exposed via the static facade. For testing or advanced multi-runtime scenarios, depend on this interface directly.

member.name: M:CodeLogic.ICodeLogicRuntime.InitializeAsync(System.Action{CodeLogic.CodeLogicOptions})
member.summary: Initializes the framework: applies options, parses CLI args, scaffolds the directory structure on first run, loads CodeLogic.json (or CodeLogic.Development.json), and creates the LibraryManager. Must be called before all other methods.
member.param: Optional delegate to configure before startup.
member.returns: An indicating success, failure, or whether the process should exit (e.g., after --version or --generate-configs).

member.name: M:CodeLogic.ICodeLogicRuntime.RegisterApplication(CodeLogic.Framework.Application.IApplication)
member.summary: Registers the consuming application with the runtime. Must be called after and before . The application's OnConfigureAsync will be called during .
member.param: The application instance to register.

member.name: M:CodeLogic.ICodeLogicRuntime.ConfigureAsync
member.summary: Configures the application: discovers DLL-based libraries, runs the application's OnConfigureAsync, generates and loads config and localization files. Must be called after and before .

member.name: M:CodeLogic.ICodeLogicRuntime.StartAsync
member.summary: Starts all registered libraries (Configure → Initialize → Start phases), then starts the application (Initialize → Start phases). Libraries are started in dependency order. Must be called after .

member.name: M:CodeLogic.ICodeLogicRuntime.StopAsync
member.summary: Gracefully stops the application first, then all plugins, then all libraries (in reverse start order). Called automatically when shutdown signals are received if is true.

member.name: M:CodeLogic.ICodeLogicRuntime.ResetAsync
member.summary: Stops everything and resets the runtime to uninitialized state. Allows calling again — useful for integration tests.

member.name: M:CodeLogic.ICodeLogicRuntime.GetHealthAsync
member.summary: Collects health status from all running libraries, plugins, and the application. Returns a with an overall IsHealthy flag.

member.name: M:CodeLogic.ICodeLogicRuntime.GetLibraryManager
member.summary: Returns the created during , or null if the runtime has not been initialized.

member.name: M:CodeLogic.ICodeLogicRuntime.GetApplication
member.summary: Returns the registered instance, or null if none registered.

member.name: M:CodeLogic.ICodeLogicRuntime.GetApplicationContext
member.summary: Returns the created during , or null if the application has not been configured yet.

member.name: M:CodeLogic.ICodeLogicRuntime.GetEventBus
member.summary: Returns the shared instance used across all libraries and the application. Always available after construction (before initialization).

member.name: M:CodeLogic.ICodeLogicRuntime.GetOptions
member.summary: Returns the applied during . Throws if not yet initialized.

member.name: M:CodeLogic.ICodeLogicRuntime.GetConfiguration
member.summary: Returns the deserialized from CodeLogic.json. Throws if not yet initialized.

member.name: M:CodeLogic.ICodeLogicRuntime.SetPluginManager(CodeLogic.Framework.Application.Plugins.PluginManager)
member.summary: Registers an app-managed PluginManager with the runtime so it participates in health checks and graceful shutdown. Call after InitializeAsync.

member.name: M:CodeLogic.ICodeLogicRuntime.GetPluginManager
member.summary: Returns the registered PluginManager, or null if none registered.

member.name: T:CodeLogic.InitializationResult
member.summary: The result of . Always check immediately after initialization — CLI flags like --version and --info set this to true.

member.name: P:CodeLogic.InitializationResult.Success
member.summary: True when initialization completed without errors. False indicates a fatal startup failure — check for details.

member.name: P:CodeLogic.InitializationResult.IsFirstRun
member.summary: True when the framework directory structure was just scaffolded for the first time. Informational — first-run scaffolding does not prevent normal startup.

member.name: P:CodeLogic.InitializationResult.ShouldExit
member.summary: True when the process should exit without continuing startup. Set by --version, --info, --generate-configs with ExitAfterGenerate=true, and initialization failures.

member.name: P:CodeLogic.InitializationResult.Message
member.summary: A human-readable description of the result. On failure, contains the error message.

member.name: P:CodeLogic.InitializationResult.RunHealthCheck
member.summary: When true, the caller should run the full startup sequence, print a health report after StartAsync(), then exit. Set by the --health CLI flag.

member.name: M:CodeLogic.InitializationResult.Succeeded(System.Boolean,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Creates a successful result. is false — startup continues normally.
member.param: True if the directory structure was just created.
True if the caller should run a health check after startup.

member.name: M:CodeLogic.InitializationResult.Failed(System.String)
member.summary: Creates a failed result. is true and is false.
member.param: A description of why initialization failed.

member.name: M:CodeLogic.InitializationResult.Exit(System.String)
member.summary: Creates a successful-but-exit result (e.g., after handling --version or --info). is true and is true.
member.param: A description of why the process should exit (e.g., "--version").
